title: Prefetch, Consumer Concurrency, Capacity, and Fairness · Contract Lens: Consumer Capacity Metric
summary: A consumer sets prefetch zero for maximum speed, starts unbounded promises, ignores message bytes and dependency pools, and scales consumers from queue depth alone. Planned artifact: Add and verify the consumer capacity metric increment in prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Consumer Capacity Metric · Theory Consumer Capacity Metric
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8600 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Consumer Capacity Metric contract for the supplied data.
